name: Sinefungin
description: Sinefungin is a solid. This compound belongs to the purine nucleosides and analogues. These are compounds comprising a purine base attached to a sugar. The proteins that adenosyl-ornithine target include RdmB, modification methylase TaqI, rRNA (adenine-N6-)-methyltransferase, and modification methylase RsrI.

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The risk or severity of QTc prolongation can be increased when Vemurafenib is combined with Sinefungin.

interactions.name: Cisapride
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cisapride can be increased when it is combined with Sinefungin.

interactions.name: Progesterone
interactions.description: The absorption of Progesterone can be decreased when combined with Sinefungin.

interactions.description: The serum concentration of Dofetilide can be increased when it is combined with Sinefungin.

interactions.name: Buspirone
interactions.description: The metabolism of Buspirone can be decreased when combined with Sinefungin.

interactions.name: Didanosine
interactions.description: Didanosine can cause a decrease in the absorption of Sinefungin resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.
